title: Interaction between ibc and inter-code tools
abstract: In general, methods for integrating intra-block copy (IBC) encoding or decoding with existing motion compensation algorithms for video encoding and decoding are described. In a representative aspect, a method for video encoding using IBC includes determining whether a current block of the current picture is to be encoded using a motion compensation algorithm, and encoding, based on the determining, the current block by selectively applying an intra-block copy to the current block. In a representative aspect, another method for video encoding using IBC includes determining whether a current block of the current picture is to be encoded using an intra-block copy, and encoding, based on the determining, the current block by selectively applying a motion compensation algorithm to the current block.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of a visual information is coded using a first coding technique; deciding, based on the determining, that a coded representation of the block being decoded excludes an indicator of use of a second coding technique; parsing the coded representation based on the deciding; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and by excluding use of a second decoding technique corresponding to the second coding technique; wherein one of the first and second coding techniques corresponds to an intra-block copy (IBC) technique that uses a second block of a same picture for coding the block being decoded and the other corresponds to an Overlapped Block Motion Compensation (OBMC) technique that uses motion information of a plurality of neighbors for coding the block being decoded. 2. The method of claim 1 , comprising decoding a second encoded block using the OBMC decoding technique by: obtaining the reference block corresponding to the block being decoded; and obtaining a motion prediction of the block being decoded, wherein the motion prediction is coded by combining motion vectors of the plurality of neighbors of the block being decoded. 3. The method of claims 1-2, comprising: when the block being decoded is decoded using the IBC decoding technique, excluding the use of the OBMC decoding technique to decode the block being decoded. 4. The method of claim 3, comprising: 

 when the reference picture is identical to a picture containing the block being decoded, excluding the use of the OBMC decoding technique to decode the block being decoded. 5. The method of claim 3, comprising: when the reference picture in a first reference list is the same as a picture containing the block being decoded, and the reference picture in a second reference list is different from the picture containing the block being decoded, using the OBMC decoding technique only on the second reference list. 6. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique; deciding, based on the determining, that a coded representation of the block being decoded excludes an indicator of use of a second coding technique; and parsing the coded representation based on the deciding; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and by excluding use of a second decoding technique corresponding to the second coding technique; wherein one of the first and second coding techniques corresponds to an intra-block copy (IBC) technique that uses a second block of a same picture for coding the block being decoded and the other corresponds to a Local Illumination Compensation (LIC) technique that uses a plurality of neighbors of a second block of a second picture and a plurality of corresponding neighbors of a reference block of a reference picture to encode an illumination change between the second block of the second picture and the reference block of the reference picture, for coding the block being decoded. 7. The method of claims 1 and 6, comprising decoding a first encoded block using the IBC decoding technique by: obtaining a first plurality of encoded blocks representing the first picture; decoding an initial block in the first plurality of encoded blocks; and 

 upon decoding the initial block, decoding a first block in the first plurality of encoded blocks based on the initial block. 8. The method of claims 6-7, comprising decoding the second block using the LIC decoding technique in a second picture by: obtaining a plurality of samples neighboring the second block and a plurality of corresponding samples in the reference picture corresponding to the second block, and parameters representing the illumination change between the plurality of samples and the plurality of corresponding samples; and calculating an illumination of the block being decoded based on the parameters, the plurality of samples and the plurality of corresponding samples. 9. The method of claims 6-8, comprising: when the block being decoded is decoded using the IBC decoding technique, excluding the use of the LIC decoding technique to decode the block being decoded. 10. The method of claims 6-9, comprising: when at least one reference picture associated with a block being decoded is the same as the picture containing the block being decoded, inferring that the LIC technique was not used to encode the block being decoded. 11. The method of claim 10, comprising: when reference pictures in both a first reference list and a second reference list associated with the block being decoded are the same as a picture containing the block being decoded, inferring that the LIC technique was not used to encode the block being decoded. 12. The method of claim 10, comprising: when reference pictures associated with the block being decoded comprise both a picture containing the block being decoded and a picture not containing the block being decoded, decoding an indicator in the plurality of indicators signaling whether the LIC technique was used 

 to encode the block being decoded, performing said decoding only when the reference picture of the block being decoded is not identical to the picture containing the block being decoded. 13. A method for encoding a visual information, comprising: encoding the visual information into a plurality of encoded pictures and a plurality of indicators signaling one or more encoding techniques applied, the plurality of indicators comprising an intra-block copy(IBC) technique and an Overlapped Block Motion Compensation (OBMC) technique, wherein a first block of a first picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the IBC technique and a second block of a second picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the OBMC technique, wherein the IBC technique uses a block of the first picture to encode a different block of the first picture and the OBMC technique uses motion information of a plurality of neighbors of the second block of the second picture to encode the second block of the second picture. 14. The method of claim 13, comprising encoding the second block using the OBMC technique in a second reference picture by: dividing the reference picture into a plurality of reference blocks including the reference block corresponding to a block being encoded; and deriving a motion prediction of the block being encoded by combining motion vectors of the plurality of neighbors of the reference block. 15. The method of claims 13-14, comprising: avoiding encoding the first picture using the OBMC technique. 16. The method of claim 15, comprising: when the reference picture is the same as the first picture, excluding the use of the OBMC technique to encode the block being encoded. 17. The method of claim 15, comprising: when the reference picture in a first reference list is the same as a picture containing the block being encoded, and the reference picture in a second reference list is different from the 

 picture containing the block being encoded, conducting the OBMC technique only on the second reference list. 18. A method for encoding a visual information, comprising: encoding the visual information into a plurality of encoded pictures and a plurality of indicators signaling one or more encoding techniques applied, the plurality of indicators comprising an intra-block copy (IBC) technique and a Local Illumination Compensation (LIC) technique, wherein a first block of a first picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the IBC technique and a second block of a second picture associated with the visual information is encoded using the LIC technique, wherein the IBC technique uses a block of the first picture to encode a different block of the first picture and the LIC technique uses a plurality of neighbors of the second block of the second picture and a plurality of corresponding neighbors of a reference block of a reference picture to encode an illumination change between the second block of the second picture and the reference block of the reference picture. 19. The method of claims 13 and 18, comprising encoding the first picture using the IBC technique by: dividing the first picture into a first plurality of blocks; encoding an initial block in the first plurality of blocks; and upon encoding the initial block, encoding a first block in the first plurality of blocks based on the initial block. 20. The method of claims 18-19, comprising encoding the second block using the LIC technique in a second picture by: determining a plurality of samples neighboring the second block and a plurality of corresponding samples in the reference picture corresponding to the second block; and deriving parameters representing the illumination change based on the plurality of samples and the plurality of corresponding samples. 21. The method of claims 18-20, comprising: avoiding encoding the first picture using the LIC technique. 22. The method of claim 21, comprising: when at least one reference picture associated with a block being encoded is the same as the picture containing the block being encoded, excluding encoding an indicator in the plurality of indicators signaling that LIC technique was used, thereby expecting a decoder to infer that the LIC technique was not used. 23. The method of claim 21, comprising: when reference pictures in both a first reference list and a second reference list associated with the block being encoded are the same as a picture containing the block being encoded, excluding encoding an indicator in the plurality of indicators signaling that the LIC technique was used, thereby expecting a decoder to infer that the LIC technique was not used. 24. The method of claim 21, comprising: when reference pictures associated with the block being encoded comprise both a picture containing the block being encoded and a picture not containing the block being encoded, encoding an indicator in the plurality of indicators signaling whether the LIC technique was used, wherein the indicator only signals whether the LIC technique is used for blocks coded without the IBC technique wherein the reference picture is not the same as a picture containing the blocks being encoded. 25. The method of claims 1-24, comprising: transmitting an indicator between an encoder and a decoder, the indicator signaling whether and how to apply the LIC technique for the IBC technique coded blocks at a sequence level, a picture level, a slice level, a Coding Tree Unit (CTU), a coding unit (CU) level, or a prediction unit (PU) level. 26. The method of claim 25, comprising: transmitting the indicator at a Sequence Parameter Set (SPS), Picture Parameter Set (PPS), a Slice Header (SH), a CTU, a CU or a PU. 27. A video processing apparatus comprising a processor configured to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 26. 28. A computer readable medium having processor-executable code stored thereon, the code, upon execution, causing a processor to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 26.
